top_categories.workout_context: Race-pace efforts that mimic Cyclassics Hamburg's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before August.
At mile 60 of 96, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
When someone attacks on paved — urban and rural German roads, fully, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Cyclassics Hamburg's paved — urban and rural German roads, fully forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 96 miles.
Cyclassics Hamburg's paved — urban and rural German roads, fully rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Cyclassics Hamburg. Train it until it feels boring.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what Cyclassics Hamburg's paved — urban and rural German roads, fully actually does to you.
2-minute all-out efforts on paved — urban and rural German roads, fully. Closing gaps, responding to attacks, sprinting for position. Train the burn.
At Cyclassics Hamburg, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Cyclassics Hamburg, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 96 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 96 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Cyclassics Hamburg’s 96 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Race Simulation, Durability, and VO2max to prepare you for 96 miles of paved — urban and rural german roads, fully closed to traffic in Hamburg, Germany.
